title: General Writing Comments (Synthetic)
description: Synthetic reusable comments demonstrating strengths, development, conventions, analysis, and revision support.

categories.label: Strengths
categories.description: Comments that identify effective choices in the writing.

categories.label: Evidence and Analysis
categories.description: Comments about support, explanation, and reasoning.

categories.label: Grammar and Conventions
categories.description: Comments about sentence-level correctness and readability.

categories.label: Revision and Next Steps
categories.description: Comments that help plan a focused revision.

categories.label: General Review
categories.description: Internal or neutral comments used to organize review.

comments.label: Clear focus
comments.text: Your response maintains a clear focus and stays connected to its central idea.
comments.teacher_note: Use when the controlling idea remains clear throughout the response.

comments.label: Evidence needs explanation
comments.text: Your evidence is relevant, but explain more clearly how it supports your central idea.
comments.teacher_note: Use when support is relevant but the reasoning remains implicit.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What does this evidence show, and why does that matter to your central idea?
comments.revision_action: Add two sentences that connect the evidence to the central idea.

comments.label: Evidence is well integrated
comments.text: You introduce and integrate evidence smoothly so it supports the flow of your explanation.

comments.label: Review sentence boundaries
comments.text: Review sentence boundaries and revise run-ons or fragments so each sentence is complete and easy to follow.
comments.revision_action: Read each sentence aloud and mark where one complete thought ends.

comments.label: Choose one revision priority
comments.text: Choose one high-impact area to revise first, then reread the full response to check how that change affects the whole piece.
comments.follow_up_prompt: Which single change would most improve this draft for your reader?
comments.revision_action: Name and complete one high-impact revision before editing smaller details.

comments.label: Meaning is unclear
comments.text: Several ideas are difficult to follow because key connections are missing or unclear.
comments.revision_action: Add the missing connection between each key idea before editing wording.

comments.label: Teacher follow-up needed
comments.text: Revisit this response after checking the assignment-specific expectations and the student's earlier draft.
comments.teacher_note: Internal reminder only; do not copy directly into student feedback.
